ip_outcome_note: Card name is 'King Piccolo, Demon Lord'. Vision confirms green-skinned figure on skeletal throne, card type line reads Namekian, imperious posture — all canonical King Piccolo markers. Named card.

trivia_bullets.text: King Piccolo (Piccolo Daimao) is the evil half of the Nameless Namekian — a single being who split himself in order to purge his impure soul and become Earth's Guardian. The good half became Kami; the evil half became King Piccolo. The two share a life link. [Dragon Ball Wiki: Nameless Namekian; CBR: 'Are Dragon Ball's Piccolo and Demon King Piccolo The Same Person?', via WebSearch snippets]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: Nameless Namekian, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Toriyama named King Piccolo after the piccolo flute, then extended that naming convention to all of his spawned sons — Piano, Cymbal, Tambourine, Drum, and others including Banjo, Bell, Viola, Conga, Harp, and Mandarin. Every member of the demon-spawn hierarchy bears a musical instrument name, establishing a deliberate labor-template: the lord names his vassals after instruments he plays. [Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo; Wikipedia: Piccolo (Dragon Ball), via WebSearch snippets]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo, via WebSearch snippet
Wikipedia: Piccolo (Dragon Ball), via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Tambourine killed Krillin in one of Dragon Ball's most brutal early deaths; Cymbal was killed and eaten by Yajirobe (cross-reference: bt4-065-dark-vassal-cymbal / dark-vassal-trio); Drum was destroyed by Goku. The instrument-named vassals function as expendable enforcers — each dispatched by the opposition in the order the narrative requires. [Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo; Dragon Ball Wiki: Cymbal, via WebSearch snippets]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo, via WebSearch snippet
Dragon Ball Wiki: Cymbal, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: King Piccolo is classified as a Namekian on the DBS card game type line (visible in the Vision section), cementing his racial identity in the card game's mechanics — despite the fact that the Namekian race was not introduced in-universe until Dragon Ball Z, years after the King Piccolo Saga originally aired. [Vision section: type line visible; Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Dragon Ball Wiki: King Piccolo, via WebSearch snippet

related_cards.note: Yajirobe killed Cymbal; the chain runs King Piccolo > Cymbal > Yajirobe
